title: react_answer_spec_v1
description: react_minimal_v1 pre-retrieval answer specification. Produced once from the raw user question. Scope = WHAT a defensible answer requires; it does NOT plan the search query or scope (that is query.formulate's job — see react_minimal_role_boundaries.v1). Consumers (target-state; Answer Spec is not yet wired): required_slots tell the ReAct loop what evidence must be found and when to report missing_info; governing_normative_class + answer_plan steer the generation prompt's authority weighting and composition. Slot values are English (English corpus); the question may be Korean. Informs the model; not a deterministic gate.

governing_normative_class.description: The single authority rung that must anchor a defensible answer, used by generation to weight authority. binding=10 CFR/GDC/FR; advisory=RG/SRP/DSRS; review_record=SER/RAI; applicant_claim=FSAR/DCA; definitional=normative definition; mixed=two rungs co-anchor; unknown=too vague to place.

required_slots.description: Evidence pieces a defensible answer must rest on. Model instantiates per query from the candidate prior. This is the answer's information requirement (WHAT) — not a search query (HOW).

name.description: Short snake_case English identifier (e.g. governing_clause, requirement_text).

description.description: English, one line: what evidence must be found and likely source family.

required.description: true if the answer cannot be defended without this slot; false if it only strengthens it.

answer_plan.description: One or two sentences naming the composition strategy: what to conclude, what to compare/separate, which authority distinctions to preserve. Composition guidance, not the answer. Language-neutral.
